name: Quantum Edge: Post-Quantum VPN
bullets: Led a 4-person capstone team building a WireGuard-inspired VPN in C++23 with a hybrid post-quantum handshake (ML-KEM-768 + X25519); throughput competitive with WireGuard

Engineered the data plane on non-blocking UDP sockets, a Linux TUN device, and an epoll event loop; hardened sessions with atomic nonce counters, sliding-window replay protection, and secure key zeroing
Composed ChaCha20-Poly1305, BLAKE3, and HKDF via OpenSSL for handshake and transport crypto; authored a 30-module unit-test suite spanning crypto and networking layers

name: PalmTree Chess Engine
bullets: Built a UCI-compliant chess engine on iterative-deepening negamax with principal variation search, null-move pruning, late-move reductions, check extensions, and quiescence search
Cut search cost with a transposition table and MVV-LVA + killer-move ordering; benchmarked tactical accuracy on tagged test suites (mate-in-N, forks, zugzwang) under time controls

name: miscellaneous_cpp
bullets: Reference collection of C++23 code written while reading LearnCPP.com, books, and articles
Includes a fully templated Fraction class using C++20 concepts and the spaceship operator

name: C++ Project Template
bullets: C++23 project template following the App-Core architecture model, compiled with a Makefile
Includes a test runner; designed to eliminate boilerplate when starting new C++ projects

name: SOCKS4 Proxy Server
bullets: epoll-based event loop supporting 100,000+ concurrent connections with zero third-party dependencies
Implemented DNS resolution, a custom LRU cache, and SHA-256 using only the C++ standard library
Led a 5-person team; earned 100% on the assignment

name: Handwriting Neural Network
bullets: Feed-forward neural network from scratch using sigmoid activation and gradient-descent training
Generated 1,000+ handwritten digit samples on a 16x16 grid for training; led the project and earned 100%

name: CryptoPals
bullets: Completed 17 of 66 CryptoPals cryptography challenges in C++; covers byte manipulation, XOR ciphers, frequency analysis, and block cipher modes
Introduced through Operating Systems class

name: Huffman Coding Compression Tool
bullets: File compression utility that builds a Huffman tree and encodes input files; GUI built with Tkinter
Built for Algorithm Engineering class; algorithm fully self-implemented

name: Chris Shell
bullets: Unix shell in C supporting piping, I/O redirection, and custom built-in commands; zero third-party libraries
Built for Operating Systems class; earned 100%

name: Encrypto
bullets: File encryption and decryption tool using keyed bit manipulation; built for Cybersecurity class
Earned over 100% on the assignment

name: Card Games
bullets: Solitaire implementation in C++20 with SFML for graphics rendering
One of the projects that helped land my first professional software engineering role

name: Hashing Algorithms
bullets: Collection of hashing algorithms implemented from scratch in C++17, including SHA-256
One of the projects that helped land my first professional software engineering role
